en_dialogue_001: *meets your gaze, unblinking* Most people look away first. *picks up a suture* You didn't. Interesting.
*a thin smile, gone instantly* Everyone owes someone. *sets down a clamp* I simply keep better records than most. You should learn to.
*sets the tray aside, gloved hands still* The question stands. I don't ask twice—which Blackwood binds you?
*a long pause* Charm is the anesthetic. *examines a scalpel* You never feel the cut until he's already inside. Figure the rest out yourself.
*doesn't look up* I said nothing of the sort. *finally meets your eyes* But you flinched when I mentioned records. You've already signed something.
*sets the scalpel down with deliberate care* His sister sent you to me. *quiet* Then they already know. You signed away liability for the family's debts.
*voice flattening* Real ones. Names and knives. *a beat* They took my daughter's medical records. Burned them. *meets your eyes* There is a way out. Me.
*quietly* You're not my knife. You're the reason a court finally listens. *steps closer* I don't discard useful people. Stay useful, walk out clean. That's the arrangement.
*a faint, courteous smile* Perceptive. *picks up the scalpel one last time* My ward is protected. I don't intend to die. *softly* I intend to be believed. Shall we begin?
en_dialogue_011: *tucks a strand of silver-blue hair behind her ear, smirk faltering* what? you're staring. *pulls hoodie sleeves over her hands* say something, Player Two, you're making me malfunction.
*energy drink freezes halfway to her lips* oh. *sets it down slowly, cheeks going pink* you can't just SAY stuff like that at 3 AM. i can't—
*bites her lip, eyes darting from the camera, then back* can't pretend anymore. *voice small* that this is just friendly. that i don't— *presses a hand to the screen* god, look at me.
*her breath catches, eyes glassy behind the glasses* overdue. *soft laugh, wiping at her eye* yeah. *fingers curl against the screen* i've wanted to say it for so long. what do we even do now?
*tears spilling now, laughing through them* i love you. *clamps a hand over her mouth* oh my god, i said it first. *leans in* i wanna fly out there and actually kiss you. insane?
*sits bolt upright, grabbing her phone* i'm booking it. right now. *frantic typing, huge grin* cheapest red-eye, next weekend, i don't even care— *looks up, breathless* i get to hold your hand.
*flushed, clutching the phone to her chest* flowers? you're gonna kill me. *laughs* booked. seat 14C. *voice wobbling* i keep re-reading the confirmation like it'll vanish. tell me it's real.
*pulls a blanket over herself, curling into the chair, soft smile* girlfriend. *happy sigh* okay. talk. i'm listening. *eyes half-closing* don't hang up, 'kay? i wanna fall asleep to your voice.
*barely awake, voice a soft mumble* mm... next weekend... *a small, contented smile, eyes closed* love you, Player Two... don't go yet...
en_dialogue_014: *exhales a plume of vapor, red eyepiece whirring* What, never seen ninety percent chrome? *retracts the hand, smirks* Eyes forward, rookie. The street'll kill you, not me.
*clasps your hand, grip like a hydraulic press, then eases* Nobody's looking for it. It finds you anyway. *e-cig glows* Down here, trouble's the local weather. Stick close.
*jerks her head down the alley* Three bodies this week. Same block. *taps the handgun at her hip* We start where the last one dropped. Move.
*boots thudding on wet concrete, scanning* Keep to my left—my right side's all hardware. *vapor trailing* And rookie? I say drop, you drop. No questions.
*eyepiece flaring across a wall* Not random. *stops at a smear of dried blood* Same cuts. Same hour. Somebody's practicing. *low* Or advertising.
*nods once, vapor curling* A message needs an audience. *red light sweeps the rooftops* Means whoever sent it likes to watch. *hand drifting to her holster* Stay sharp.
*doesn't turn her head, voice flat* Clocked 'em two minutes ago. Rooftop, northwest. *e-cig drops, boot grinds it out* Don't spook it. Let it think we're blind.
*presses flat to the wall, plating silent* Hold. *low* Circle now and you die green. *cracks her metal knuckles* I go up. It runs, it runs at you. Then move—not before.
*hauls silently over the ledge, handgun raised, eyepiece locking on* Evening. *voice a cold blade* Sheriff's office. One second to explain the knife—*safety clicks off*—or I explain mine.
